title: Any other Mari haters out there
display_title: Any other Mari haters out there
wikitext:
[]: Any other Mari haters out there? Post by palehorseXIII » Mon Aug 22, 2011 2:19 am To me it seems like her whole character is just there for fan service and nothing else. Everything she actually does could be done by Asuka and often was in the series. She seems like a rather pointless addition.

[]: Post by Tarnsman » Mon Aug 22, 2011 2:22 am Mari is hot and makes me happy in my pants. Therefore her entire character is justified. I hope Mari has absolutely no purpose in the greater scheme of things and is just a "pointless fanservice character" who gets no back story beyond little hints of information that are never expanded on, and has zero character development.

[]: Post by Chrad » Mon Aug 22, 2011 3:59 am Thread title made me laugh. You won't need to look far to find Mari haters.

[]: Post by Xous » Mon Aug 22, 2011 5:12 am While I do agree her role in 2.22 was rather lacking (though to me that made her really mysterious, which I think was at least part of the point of her character), that's what the remaining films are for, right? I think it's really hard to say her addition to the series was pointless when this iteration of the series is only halfway over. Though there's always the chance they'll give her the same treatment in the remaining films, I suppose...

[]: Post by SaltyJoe » Mon Aug 22, 2011 6:06 am I don't dislike the character, in fact, i don't have that much of an opinion on her. But the background information that was revealed about how her character was conceived really left a sour taste in my mouth. An exceprt from this interview: Enokido: Concerning the matter of strengthening Mari's character, Anno-san had already been asking me if I had any ideas, so I started to investigate the problem. However, as I worked through it, the twosome "Rei and Asuka" was such a powerful combination that they seemed to stand in the way. When I tried to investigate what made this pair so strong, I realized that this combination followed the archetype of the so called "harem anime," and that all the desires, lusts, and dreams of young men were bound up in them.

[]: Post by Jayfive » Mon Aug 22, 2011 7:38 am I think its too early to say one way of the other. Lets see who she is and what she does first. I mean, did you all come to a unwavering conclusion about Asuka before you watching Ep22?

[]: Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Aug 22, 2011 11:40 am I like how Mari fails at battles despite being so gung-ho about them (Shinji's opposite in that way, compared to NGE (but not a 1:1 comparison) between him and Asuka Soryu). CRC Interviews state Mari was setup to be fleshed out in 3.0 (whereas 2.0 merely introduces her (and Asuka Shikinami?) ) so I'm going to wait for that before passing judgement on her. For now I find it interesting she's so mysterious. And I can't wait to find out what's the deal between her, Kaji and the IPEA . I hope Mari won't be some combination of "left-over" NGE aspects of Soryu or Rei but she is shaping up to be her own character in her own right from what little we've seen of her. Mari's very first battle as well as the scene where she's spacing out and staring at those birds come to mind. It doesn't strike me as the kind of contemplation either Rei or Asuka (Soryu or what little we've so-far seen of Shikinami) are prone to. Maybe I just want to have faith in Studio Khara, but I think faith in them is justified given how NGE (EoE included) turned out. At least, what one person on the production team says (eg: Enokido) doesn't necessarily reflect Anno's own thoughts, which may or may not be helpful.

[]: Mari-devil girl. B/ Post by Alaska Slim » Mon Aug 22, 2011 11:43 am The parts of the same interview Joe listed disturb me in how much of the original plan for Mari involved outright replacing Asuka and pushing her to the sidelines, not just as an Eva pilot for one fight, but all the fights, and as the primary love interest for Shinji. But how they decided to re-tool her character intrigues me, that of being what Anno intends to "destroy Eva", which I can only infer means at other points in the story she'll be a guiding hand (no pun intended) to the other characters, so that they make the "right" choice. But even that won't stand up if we don't get more of a character arch from her. She either has to evolve and change herself, or a light has to be shed on her backstory to explain her motivations.

[]: Post by palehorseXIII » Mon Aug 22, 2011 11:59 am I'll give her a fair chance in future movies. But so far I'm just not seeing much to like personally.

[]: Post by Charsiupao » Mon Aug 22, 2011 12:56 pm When I first saw that they were adding a new pilot I was quite upset - I mean in the series, Rei and Asuka were pretty much a perfect foil. Having watched 2.22 Mari has wormed her way into my good books, though. For me, it was just the way she was singing while driving that pile of junk down the tunnel to engage Tunniel, how excited she was to be in an Eva, even a provisional one, they way she riposted Kaji's regret on using kids to further his agenda, and the way she bade Eva-05 farewell. This is a girl who enjoys piloting, knows what she's doing, is chirpy and optimistic about life (which is refreshing compared to the other three), respects the Eva (whether it's a provisional pile of junk or a state-of-of-the-art hijacked model) and has a mysterious air about knowing more than she should. The character wasn't developed enough, though, and you don't see her talking to anyone other than Shinji (which didn't tell much about her) and her employer in Acheron (which showed her enthusiasm). She has the confidence and ability of an Asuka, minus the arrogance and the chip on the shoulder (as far as can be seen), and in the battle with Zeruel did the Eva-02 far more justice than Asuka did in the series. She lost, but that was because the Eva-02 was seriously outmatched by Zeruel, not because of any poor tactics by the pilot. I have mixed feelings about replacing Kaji's famous watermelon scene with Mari, but she handled that scene pretty well. It would have been a mistake to have her take the same angle that Kaji did, and her view of the situation was just as real and relevant, and had the same effect. Although she did that scene well, I still miss Kaji's watermelon scene, which was one of the best scenes in NGE for me. At least they had a tribute to the watermelon patch! The next movie will flesh her out more and then maybe I'll know if she's someone i really like, or just another filler to capitalise on fans fetish for young girls in plugsuits (although in terms of fanservice I don't find her as attractive as Rei or Asuka). From the little I saw in 2.22, she's much more likeable than I expected her to be. Hope she doens't end up falling for Shinji and forming another corner of a love triangle, or become a villain and betrays everyone or something. If Tabris is a villain then hopefully her assistance will help in fighting him back or something.

[]: Post by Someone » Mon Aug 22, 2011 1:25 pm Yes:

[]: Post by Ricky O » Mon Aug 22, 2011 1:39 pm Nope! :

[]: Post by AuraTwilight » Mon Aug 22, 2011 2:54 pm There's not enough THERE to hate. I can't hate Mari any more than I can hate a lamp.

[]: Post by Gendo'sPapa » Mon Aug 22, 2011 2:57 pm I have the same issues with her as I have with Rebuild's version of Kaworu. Which is to say I have NO issues. Mari & Kaworu haven't yet played their role in the Rebuild. Both characters are around for a reason but the main function of their characters existence has yet to occur.

[]: Post by KnightmareX13 » Mon Aug 22, 2011 4:01 pm I like her, but that may change at the end of Rebuild.

[]: Re: Any other Mari haters out there? Post by Hyper Shinchan » Mon Aug 22, 2011 8:06 pm palehorseXIII wrote: To me it seems like her whole character is just there for fan service and nothing else. Everything she actually does could be done by Asuka and often was in the series. She seems like a rather pointless addition.

[]: Post by Archaiel » Mon Aug 22, 2011 8:08 pm She intrigues me, but I cannot say I like or dislike her. Mari isn't fleshed out yet so I am withholding judgement.

[]: Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Mon Aug 22, 2011 8:59 pm Hate threads; because that's exactly what this forum needs... Whereas I see everyone's complaint that there's not much to like about Mari, there's an equal amount lacking in elements to dislike about the character. I mean, seriously. She shows up in the series well-endowed, then completely confuses everyone in her epic battles at the end. From there you're supposed to decide if you love the character or hate the character. As a fighter, she seemed amazing despite the fact she failed. As a teenage girl she seems to be a very attractive teenage girl. As a character she seems to have her own goals and her own hidden agendas, probably even those Kaji isn't fully aware of. (Unless they made a pact, but then we're going off into speculation.) Basically, I don't know enough about the character to have an opinion outside of her fighting style and her weird knowledge and abilities of the Evas. And she seemed pretty good in both aspects. (Just how many people outside of Mari and Ritsuko knew about "Beast Mode" anyway?) SaltyJoe wrote: But the background information that was revealed about how her character was conceived really left a sour taste in my mouth. An exceprt from this interview:

[]: Post by Hyper Shinchan » Mon Aug 22, 2011 9:57 pm FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: Hate threads; because that's exactly what this forum needs...

[]: Post by BC Baron » Mon Aug 22, 2011 10:50 pm SaltyJoe wrote: ...how about, say, a short, balding, middle aged fat guy, who is naturally not an Eva pilot, playing a role in the plot, instead of yet another hot chick working the robots?
